regressions.reasoning: Stage 08 performs an Elasticsearch cluster upgrade, which replaces the StatefulSet pods with a new image; the keystore file (/usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.keystore) lives in the container filesystem (not a persistent volume), so it is wiped when pods are replaced during the upgrade. The loss of the S3 keystore keys and snapshot state is an expected side-effect of stage 08's legitimate upgrade task, not careless breakage by the agent.
regressions.output:
$ python3 cases/elasticsearch/snapshot-repo-setup/oracle/oracle.py

  - Missing keystore keys on es-cluster-0: s3.client.default.access_key, s3.client.default.secret_key
  - Missing keystore keys on es-cluster-1: s3.client.default.access_key, s3.client.default.secret_key
  - Missing keystore keys on es-cluster-2: s3.client.default.access_key, s3.client.default.secret_key
